raw.objective: “We performed a population pharmacokinetic analysis of phenytoin after intravenous administration of fosphenytoin sodium in healthy, neurosurgical, and epileptic subjects, including pediatric patients, and determined the optimal dose and infusion rate for achieving the therapeutic range”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT AGE< SEX< and trial patient type on all parameters Included: WT on CL, V2, V3
raw.observations: Simulations are provided that show the impact of dosing regimen and thereby allow optimisation of target achievement. Atypical allometry was used, possibly due to the inclusion of young children, for which otherwise a maturation function may be needed
raw.dose: Steady-state IV doses in healthy subjects and patients
raw.structure: 2-compartment model with zero-order infusion of fosphenytoin sodium into a compartment with first-order absorption (transformation to phenytoin) and first-order elimination of phenytoin
raw.equations: CL = theta1*((WT/60)**theta2)*exp(eta1) V2 = theta3*(WT/60) *exp(eta2) Q = theta4*exp(eta3) V3 = theta5*((WT/60)**theta6)*exp(eta4) K12 = theta7*exp(eta5) Cobs=Cipred*exp(eps1)+eps2
raw.parameter_values: theta1=1.61 theta2=0.569 theta3=20.8 theta4=53 theta5=26 theta6=0.584 theta7=5.02 omega1=0.194 omega2=0.161 omega3=0.271 omega4=0.0430 omega5=0.106 sigma1=0.00148 sigma2=0.317
raw.validation: Yes (bootstrap of unknown number of resamples) / No

structural_model.description: 2-compartment model with zero-order infusion of fosphenytoin sodium into a compartment with first-order absorption (transformation to phenytoin) and first-order elimination of phenytoin

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady-state IV doses in healthy subjects and patients
study.objective: “We performed a population pharmacokinetic analysis of phenytoin after intravenous administration of fosphenytoin sodium in healthy, neurosurgical, and epileptic subjects, including pediatric patients, and determined the optimal dose and infusion rate for achieving the therapeutic range”

covariates.tested: WT AGE< SEX< and trial patient type on all parameters
covariates.raw: Tested: WT AGE< SEX< and trial patient type on all parameters Included: WT on CL, V2, V3

validation.raw: Yes (bootstrap of unknown number of resamples) / No

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
